core_model.structure_summary: Minimization of a weighted average, over distributed units, of local objectives each combining a log-loss between a normalized exponential output map and reference labels with a divergence term pulling the current map toward a composite target distribution built by rescaling a frozen earlier map's normalized outputs by one minus the mass the current map assigns to newly added coordinates.
core_model.canonical_form: \min_{W} \sum_k \omega_k J^k(W), \quad J^k = \mathbb{E}\big[ L_{CE}(\sigma(f(W;x)), y) + \beta\, D_{KL}(\sigma(f(W;x)) \,\|\, z) \big], \qquad z_j = \begin{cases} \sigma(f(W;x)/\theta)_j, & j \in \mathcal{N} \\ \sigma(f(W_{0};x)/\theta)_j \cdot \Big(1 - \sum_{c \in \mathcal{N}} \sigma(f(W;x)/\theta)_c\Big), & j \notin \mathcal{N} \end{cases}

variables.meaning: parameter vector of the map being optimized
frozen parameter vector of an earlier map used to build the composite target
normalized nonnegative output vector on the simplex produced by exponentiating and normalizing the map's outputs
scaling factor applied inside the exponential normalization and a weight balancing the two loss terms
weighted sum of expected log-loss and divergence terms across distributed units

deterministic_skeleton.summary: Minimization of an expected divergence between a parametric distribution on a simplex and a reference distribution assembled by the law of total probability from a fixed distribution on a coordinate subset and the current distribution's mass on the complementary subset.

notes: The theoretical core is a total-probability decomposition: outputs of the frozen map are interpreted as probabilities conditional on the complement of the new coordinate set, so multiplying them by (1 - mass on new coordinates) recovers unconditional probabilities; the composite target then feeds a divergence-regularized objective. mean_field_coupling refers to the global objective being a weighted average over all distributed units. Several extracted equations are truncated (extraction noise), but the objective and target construction are unambiguous.
